items.rationale: Sees stablecoins as found product-market fit and the 'WhatsApp moment for money' — open, instant, borderless rails that bypass extractive intermediaries and entrench US dollar dominance.

citations.quote: Stablecoins are our first real shot at doing for money what email did for communication: make it open, instant, and borderless.
It's time to pass bipartisan stablecoin legislation. The GENIUS Act will protect consumers and increase transparency
This stablecoin bill protects consumers and gives entrepreneurs the guardrails they need to drive innovation and economic growth

items.rationale: Believes blockchains supply what AI lacks — ownership, identity/proof-of-human, authenticity, trustworthy human feedback data — and repeatedly invests at this intersection.

citations.quote: I wrote about why AI needs blockchain-enabled computing, and why I'm optimistic as these technologies converge.
AI needs robust and trustworthy human data. Crypto is built to provide it.

items.rationale: Sees autonomous AI agents as a new class of economic actor needing native financial rails; backed Catena Labs to let agents transact in stablecoins instantly.

citations.quote: Machine-speed systems need machine-speed money. Catena is building the missing link, and we're excited to back them.

items.rationale: Views DeFi as growing and popular but still too complex; excited about products that simplify it to bring the next 100M users onchain.

citations.quote: leading the $15M seed round in @legendapp to simplify DeFi and help bring the next 100M users onchain.
DeFi remains popular — and it's growing

items.rationale: Argues generative AI breaks the creator-platform economic covenant; excited about onchain IP registration so creators get attributed and compensated.

citations.quote: We want to help Story become the infrastructure for AI systems and creative people to happily co-exist.

items.rationale: Believes open source is underfunded and over-reliant on volunteers; excited about protocols that attribute and reward contributors so repos become organizing units for networks.

citations.quote: Merit is building a protocol that attributes and rewards contributors proportionally to the value they create.

items.rationale: Considers identity verification a necessary standard in the AI age to isolate bots and imposters while protecting privacy; expanded investment in World.

citations.quote: World has made outstanding progress in advancing Proof of Human -- a necessary technology standard in the age of AI

items.rationale: Treats forward-looking legislation (GENIUS, CLARITY, FIT21, market structure) as the key unlock that moves crypto from sandbox to infrastructure and keeps building in the US.
items.age_note: Theme persistent across 2024–2025; intensity rose sharply in 2025 as bills advanced.

citations.quote: Now we need comprehensive market structure legislation so the US can lead in building blockchain networks.
smart legislation is actually the unlock... Clear rules of the road for stablecoins and crypto market structure could finally allow these technologies to move out of the sandbox

items.rationale: Sees major scaling upgrades having drastically cut transaction costs, making infrastructure finally ready for consumer applications to flourish.

citations.quote: Major scaling upgrades have drastically reduced onchain transaction costs
Blockchain infra is now ready for apps to flourish

items.rationale: Worries AI search and AI-generated content consume human creators' work without attribution or compensation, destroying incentives to publish original work online.

citations.quote: If there's no attribution or compensation, what incentive will there be to publish original creations on the open internet?
AI is already upending the internet's economic covenant, and big tech companies are best-placed to reap the rewards.

items.rationale: Fears AI advances concentrate rewards in a small number of big incumbent platforms, sidelining little tech, startups, and creators.

citations.quote: big tech companies are best-placed to reap the rewards

items.rationale: Concerned AI-powered deepfakes and bots are degrading trust in the online world; sees blockchain identity/authenticity as the remedy.

citations.quote: AI-powered deepfakes and bots are also degrading people's trust in the online world.

items.rationale: Opposes rules that would effectively ban open-source and small-developer AI models, which he sees as ceding ground to closed big-company and adversary models.
items.age_note: Stated Jan 2025 re: a specific last-minute rule; not restated after, so partly tied to that moment.

citations.quote: Another terrible last minute rule- a de facto to ban on open source and little tech AI models.

items.rationale: Critiques a regulatory regime that lets only memecoins thrive while productive tokens face hurdles, and the harmful casino culture that undermines crypto's reputation.
items.age_note: Core critique from the book (Jan 2024) and April 2024 FT oped; not directly restated in 2025, though the regulatory fix he sought has been advancing.

citations.quote: the backward incentives of a regulatory regime in the US that lets only memecoins thrive — while companies and tokens with more productive uses face hurdles.

items.rationale: Views fiat-backed stablecoins as commodity-like, near-free, instant global money rails — the leading edge of crypto crossing into real infrastructure; expects issuers to have weak pricing power.

citations.quote: Stablecoins from different issuers are commodities & cheaply interchangeable... issuers will have very weak market power
In 2024, stablecoins moved $15.6 trillion in value, effectively matching Visa's volume.

items.rationale: Sees memecoins as emblematic of the speculative casino culture that distorts incentives and harms crypto's public perception, even as he declines to outright condemn them.
items.age_note: From April 2024 FT oped and book; not restated in 2025.

citations.quote: My goal isn't to defend or to diminish memecoins. It's to point out the backward incentives of a regulatory regime

risk_regime.rationale: Across the most recent statements Dixon is emphatically bullish: he describes crypto advancing on multiple fronts, infrastructure finally ready for apps, stablecoins hitting PMF, and a decisive pro-crypto regulatory turn (EO, SEC task force, GENIUS/CLARITY/market-structure progress). He frames the moment as crypto crossing the chasm from experiment to infrastructure backbone, with deployment of fresh capital into seed rounds throughout 2025.

notes: View is overwhelmingly structural/long-horizon and builder-oriented; Dixon speaks in protocols, sectors, and policy rather than liquid token tickers, so token-level calls are thin (stablecoins as an asset class is the clearest, plus implicit equity/protocol bets like Story, World, Merit, Legend, Catena, Yupp). Much of early-2024 corpus is book-promotion (Read Write Own) and event logistics with little market signal. The dominant 2025 throughline is twofold: (1) crypto×AI as the defining opportunity — ownership, identity, IP, agent payments, and human-feedback data — and (2) US regulatory clarity as the unlock. His concerns are largely the mirror image of his theses (AI value-capture by Big Tech, lost creator incentives, eroded trust), positioning crypto as the fix. The memecoin/casino-culture critique is genuine but rooted in 2024 statements and not restated in 2025, likely because the regulatory remedy he advocated has been advancing.
